content: I will modify `article-divorce-without-consent.html` to wrap the copied content inside a full, valid HTML document structure (`<!DOCTYPE html><html><head>...<body>`). This triggers Microsoft Word's standard HTML document import engine, which correctly applies all stylesheet classes (such as Times New Roman and page-like indents) to the pasted content.
thinking:
**Refining HTML Integration**

Okay, I've decided to tackle implementing the HTML document wrapper by rewriting lines 948 to 1104 in `article-divorce-without-consent.html`. I'm going to carefully craft the new code block. I've started defining a `copyBtn` variable now.

args.Description: "Wrapped clipboard HTML in a fully structured HTML document (<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><body>) with general body font configurations to force Microsoft Word's HTML importer to apply Times New Roman, page indents, table signatures, and correct list formats."
args.Instruction: "Wrap clipboard HTML text in standard HTML/head/body tags and style the body tag directly with Times New Roman and size 12pt for Word compatibility."
args.toolAction: "Wrapping clipboard HTML in full HTML document"
